calendar.matters:
Mtn For Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Oct-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: NANCY TAVERNIT, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, 415.554.3800, FOR RESPONDENT RETIREMENT BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYEE'S RETIREMENT SYSTEM; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PETITIONER BRIAN WILLIAMS' Motion For Writ Of Mandamus is denied. Per the independent judgment standard yet giving a presumption of correctness to the ALJ's finidings, the weight of the evidence supports the determination that Petitioner did not suffer from an industrially-related physical condition that rendered him substantially unable to perform the usual duties of his position. Although the specifics of SFPD's offer of modified light duty in August/September 2009 are not entirely clear, Petitioner had been previously been assigned to modified light duty. Faced with returning to work, Petitioner made no further inquiry regarding the light duty assignment and categorically refused to return to work based on his own subjective belief of his unfitness, which was not substantiated by the medical evidence. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Mtn For Writ Of Mandamus
Off Calendar Oct-10-2012 Continued To Oct-17-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
PETITIONER BRIAN WILLIAMS' motion For Writ Of Mandamus is continued on the court's own motion to October 17, 2012. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(302/HEK)
